prompt: A young child, maybe 7 or 8 years old, climbing and playing on the rusted hull of a large fallen missile or artillery shell half-buried in dry cracked earth. Open barren landscape, overcast flat light. Sheep grazing in the far distance. The child looks curious and playful, completely unbothered — this is just their playground. No blood, no fire, no war imagery. The horror is in the normalcy. Photojournalistic style, desaturated, wide shot, large format documentary photography. Dust and rust. Silence.
dataset_name: Stop the War on Children – Children in Conflict Data
insight: In 2024, one in five children globally — more than 500 million — lived in a conflict zone, the highest share ever recorded.
